name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: "Drop of Water in a Big Blue Wave." (please read)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger For Congress
body:
After our recent fundraising efforts to ready ourselves for 2020, I hope you'll enjoy reflecting on what we did in 2018!
| | Peter --

We are months past our election, and yet friends and supporters continue to share stories of how our race impacted them. One of our supporters wrote this piece to share her experience volunteering with our campaign. It is a long, but wonderful read outlining what Karen called her "rite of passage", and I'd love to share a story her story of "evolving from a dispassionate, usually Republican voter to a committed volunteer and Democratic activist."

After our recent fundraising efforts to ready ourselves for 2020, I hope you'll enjoy reflecting on what we did in 2018!

Enjoy this read!

_-Abigail_ | "Yay, it's a Spanberger person!"

The young woman who opened her front door, a purring kitten nestled to her chest in a baby carrier, was excited to see me. I was more than halfway through my list of 50 doors to knock for Abigail Spanberger, the Democratic candidate running for Congress in my central Virginia district. After so many unanswered entreaties, I was eager to hear this special needs teacher describe her fear of losing funding for her students; how she earned only $15 an hour ($15.50 if you count overtime) and who ultimately asked if she could have a "really big Abigail yard sign."

Me, a "Spanberger person?" How did that happen? How did I come to knock on almost 700 doors, donate to my first political campaign and blow up balloons for the candidate at a local community fair? When one homeowner asked if I was a Democrat, I stammered, "Well, guess I am now."

It's only fitting that I had become our latest cultural cliche: the educated, white suburban woman, desperate to be for someone instead of simply against a reality TV president who.....well, I'll stop there before I really get going.

My life has been a series of cliches--from the 30-year-old professional single woman whom Newsweek in 1986 doomed to a life of spinsterhood (sigh), to a soccer mom in a mini-van a decade later (another sigh).

However, this time felt different. The cliche wasn't shaping me; I was shaping it. I was a drop of water in the big blue wave sweeping the nation and it felt good. Walking from door to door in neighborhoods I didn't know existed gave me time to contemplate more than just esoteric house numbering systems.

I had always considered myself socially liberal yet fiscally conservative. That usually, but not always, translated into voting Republican. Until a year ago, I didn't know that my family lived in Virginia's 7th Congressional District-- or VA07 as I now know to call it. I didn't vote in the 2014 Republican primary when my representative, Dave Brat, ousted Majority Leader Eric Cantor. Primaries were for partisans, not regular voters like me.

So I was bemused to find myself walking in that first neighborhood, far removed from my home but only a few miles away. My starting point, however, was acutely clear.

Our younger daughter, Emily, had moved home after graduating from the University of Virginia. (Another cliche: Boomerang parent) She was distressed about the Charlottesville white supremacy rally and wanted to attend the candlelight vigil that followed. I didn't want her to go alone so I went too. She started actively campaigning for the 2017 candidates. Again, to be supportive, I went canvassing with her. We went to a victory party together in November. It was fun to be on the winning team.

Soon after, Emily moved onward and out of the house (yay!) and I became an "MSNBC Mom" watching Joe Scarborough and Rachel Maddow far too much and fuming at President Trump's latest acts of mendacity and a feckless Republican Party. I even started falling asleep to reruns of West Wing on Netflix--all 156 episodes-- because I craved "witty political discourse." Even the fictional kind.

Less than two months later, I was meeting a candidate for Congress in a small ranch house with, perhaps, just 20 other people. Abigail Spanberger stood in front of the fireplace and spoke for a few minutes. She was calm, smart, and dedicated to working across the aisle. I signed up to volunteer, not really appreciating what that would mean.

I quickly found out.

An "Abigail Spanberger for Congress" yard sign was soon delivered to my house. Now that I was "out" in my very red neighborhood, I figured I had to do more. I wrote a check for $250-my first ever. I agreed to knock on doors and met MacKenzie, my field organizer. It was so cool to have her phone number in my contact list.

After Abigail (we were now on a first name basis) won the Democratic primary, we went to her victory party and a New York Times photographer took my picture with the candidate. I proudly posted it to Facebook with the comment, " I would have preferred a byline for my first appearance in the NYTimes. But I'll settle for a photo that only I would recognize." At least my hair looked good!

Back on home front, Congressman Brat, a far-right Freedom Caucus member, was bemoaning women who "got up in his grill" and refusing to hold town halls. On the surface, it was a choice between a man who was afraid of soccer-moms-asking-questions and a woman who had hunted down terrorists for the CIA.

I was now posting anodyne campaign notices on Facebook, even though I disliked it when others did the same for their own political favorites. When Abigail appeared at a local brewery, I mined my "likes" and then offered local friends a yard sign. I went to other "meet and greets." I wrote another check--this one for $1,000.

It was now getting real.

I had recently read David McCullough's biography, Truman, and was struck by the power of President Truman's personal letters. Inspired, I penned three-page, hand-written missives to friends and family who lived in "safe" Democratic districts across the country. "Your representative needs your vote--but Abigail needs your money. So I don't feel guilty asking." My little local contest could have national implications.

Week after week, I walked and knocked. I was often greeted by boisterous dogs and folks more interested in talking about potholes than politics. There were many unanswered doors; a few "abruptly closed." But not many. Every time I was tempted to skip the hard-to-find house, I told myself that just one vote could make a difference.

My affluent suburban bubble was far more diverse than I had ever realized.

The weekend Hurricane Florence threatened, but missed central Virginia, everyone had hunkered down and was home when I came to call. I spent four hours, twice the usual time to cover 50 homes. When one man said that he'd been a life-long Republican until 2016 and desperately wanted leaders who told the truth, I felt we really had a chance to color this solidly red district, blue.

And then there was the older gentlemen whose house cleaner answered the door, telling me he was in the backyard. I was tempted to just leave my literature. But it was a nice day, so I went back and chatted with a lovely man who asked, unprompted, "Can I give you some money?" He wrote a check for $250. For Abigail. I was blown away, giddy to hand over the check to the campaign staff.

I was now spending lots of time at campaign headquarters. Even though I was just one of thousands of volunteers, the staff always made me feel special--like the bar in Cheers where everybody knew my name. (Lots of snacks but no beer.) My husband manned the phones and I brought the staff lunch. Twice. I hardly cook at home but I grilled chicken for Caesar salads at 8 am. When I was asked to train new phone bank callers one day, I felt honored.

And then it was over. We had won by 6,784 votes, close to the same number of registered volunteers. How fitting. I stopped by the office to pick up chairs I had lent the campaign and say good bye to people I probably won't ever see again.

Since the election, I've wondered if I would again volunteer for Abigail if a moderate Republican ran against her in 2020.

Absolutely.

It's because I was invited inside, to be part of something bigger than myself, with people who worked extremely hard to elect the most competent person possible.

To paraphrase one of my favorite West Wing characters, Ainsley Hayes, the Republican lawyer hired by Democratic President Bartlet:

"The people I have met have been extraordinarily qualified. Their intent is good. Their commitment is true. They are righteous and they are patriots. And I am their voter." (Season 2; Episode 4)

_Bio:

Karen Soule is a Contributing Writer for Chesapeake Bay Magazine and a newly-activated Democratic activist in Richmond, VA._

I was proud to introduce historic legislation to require background checks on every firearm purchase - and last month, my House colleagues answered the call of the American people and passed this important measure.

Now, Senator Mitch McConnell won't even bring it to a vote in the Senate.

**Americans have made it very clear that they support universal background checks.[Will you add your name to our petition and demand McConnell bring background checks up for a vote in the Senate?](https://act.myngp.com/el/plq0gSWpauoLUQoD087dbI5KfAiDbyEJsyZaAaFuNyE=/NAWa__4RUXfAS2qWNwIcOFxhyf2GgJtmMRsPhWY7Dk0=)**

I've worked hard with colleagues on both sides of the aisle to pass this important legislation to make our communities safer from the threat of gun violence.

We can't let McConnell be the roadblock on our path to the first serious action on common sense gun violence prevention by Congress in 25 years.

Mike

name: Jason Yates
subject: The response has been overwhelming
body:
| | Peter, The film _Unplanned_ has only been in theaters for one week and it is already making a huge impact! Read just a few of the comments from viewers moved by this film who wrote to My Faith Votes. _Thanks for the heads up on a game changer of a movie! I was pro-choice, because I thought the government shouldn’t be involved in the rights of a woman’s body. But now I clearly see the rights of every unborn child is more important. Even Jesus talked about our children as being the most precious on earth among humanity, yet we are killing more than in the holocaust! –**Chad**_ _I took my 2 teenagers to see it last Friday and I will never forget the impact that it had on them and myself! Very well done and it gave us the opportunity to have a really meaningful conversation about this ultra-important topic. My gratitude goes to the actors and directors of this film, that they were brave enough to make available to us the incredible testimony of Abby Johnson's experience. -**Monica B.**_ _Thought it was wonderfully done, hard-hitting, truth. I would challenge everyone to see it no matter what side of this issue you are on. -**Elise P.**_ If you haven’t seen the film yet, we strongly encourage you to go see the movie. It’s been added to 700 more theaters this week! Find a [theater near you](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=e2tJmdOoZ5X04TZHkoqVveqXoqSb9oEKoOK7hFKtNSU&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190405com01&n=1). Then consider taking action. View the [My Faith Votes resources](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iRKrXl6C43_CXRig9Aqf50hly8twRc4hawrtxCIG2d3eG9jzew4qXGydHIMyabIJpg&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190405com01&n=2) to help you get involved with the pro-life cause. [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iRKrXl6C43_CXRig9Aqf50hly8twRc4hawrtxCIG2d3eG9jzew4qXGydHIMyabIJpg&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190405com01&n=3) **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO

I was pro-choice, because I thought the government shouldn’t be involved in the rights of a woman’s body. But now I clearly see the rights of every unborn child is more important. Even Jesus talked about our children as being the most precious on earth among humanity, yet we are killing more than in the holocaust! –Chad I took my 2 teenagers to see it last Friday and I will never forget the impact that it had on them and myself! Very well done and it gave us the opportunity to have a really meaningful conversation about this ultra-important topic. My gratitude goes to the actors and directors of this film, that they were brave enough to make available to us the incredible testimony of Abby Johnson's experience. -Monica B. Thought it was wonderfully done, hard-hitting, truth. I would challenge everyone to see it no matter what side of this issue you are on. -Elise P. If you haven’t seen the film yet, we strongly encourage you to go see the movie. It’s been added to 700 more theaters this week! Find a . Then consider taking action. View the to help you get involved with the pro-life cause. United. We Stand. Jason Yates CEO * ©2019 My Faith Votes 10940 S Parker, 640, Parker, CO 80134

Hello there,
I'm reaching out about our end-of-quarter numbers before too much time goes by.
The last few days of March were incredible -- we saw a huge surge in support from this grassroots community. Thank you.
Unfortunately, we still finished a bit behind where we needed to be. If we're going to make up ground, we need to start April with a little more momentum than initially planned. With your support, that won't be a problem.
[**$3 or more would help us kick off April with the funds we need to close the gap. Will you please give $3 (or as much as makes sense for you) to help us start the month off strong?**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc283/2a380d31/3b433d9d/f11b10/3069130750/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuX2dlbmVyYWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE5MDQwNF9UaGFua1lvdV82bW8iLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan_general?refcode=e20190404_ThankYou_6mo&amounts=3,25,50,100,250&recurring=auto")
We only won our 2016 race by just over a thousand votes. I cannot underscore enough that every goal that we hit -- and every goal that we miss -- could be the thing that wins us reelection or the thing that hands this seat back to Republicans.

Thank you very much.
With every good wish,
Maggie Hassan

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Hiring Speeds Up as U.S. Economy Adds 196,000 Jobs in March
body:
America’s economy beat market expectations once again and created 196,000 new jobs last month, Jeffry Bartash reports for MarketWatch. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a1ede295be&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Hiring Speeds Up as U.S. Economy Adds 196,000 Jobs in March**

America’s economy beat market expectations once again and created 196,000 new jobs last month, Jeffry Bartash reports for _MarketWatch_.

“Hiring increased in most major segments of the economy,” Bartash adds. “The flush of new jobs kept the unemployment rate near a 50-year low of 3.8% . . . [and] wages are rising near the fastest pace in a decade.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6e0904af2f&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “The number of Americans filing applications for new unemployment benefits fell last week to the lowest level in half a century,” [Harriet Torry and Paul Kiernan](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=faf84c9f82&e=3ee4985eea) report for _The Wall Street Journal_. “Thursday’s report suggests the labor market remains strong, leaving businesses reluctant to let workers go.”
---
|
---
| “Proving themselves happy to pander to folks who can’t let go of the Collusion Delusion, The New York Times and Washington Post are both suggesting that Attorney General William Barr spun special counsel Robert Mueller’s report to make President Trump look good. But neither account amounts to more than clickbait,” the _[New York Post](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3585d66ce5&e=3ee4985eea)_ editorial board writes. “Much of the news media, and many top Democrats, already have egg on their faces, thanks to Mueller’s findings. It’s astounding that they’re trying to recover by digging the hole deeper.”
---
|
---
| “U.S. small businesses continued their aggressive hunt for new workers and added employees at a near-record pace in March. That’s according to the latest employment report from the National Federation of Independent Business,” James Freeman writes in _[The Wall Street Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=848ccea367&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Also encouraging in this latest NFIB survey is that owners report robust hiring plans.”
---
|
---
| “There has been a 50 percent jump in illegal immigrant gang members coming into the United States this year, according to the former Border Patrol chief,” Paul Bedard writes in the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=84a6a482a7&e=3ee4985eea)_. “What’s more, he warned in Senate testimony Thursday, loopholes in federal law will result in some 650,000 illegal immigrants — more than the population of Wyoming — being released into the United States,” where most will fail to appear for immigration hearings.
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e0ca97eaa8&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5a80a24712&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=dd1d89fb1a)
---
| | | The White House * 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW * Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
---

| | Ever since Democrats took back the House, they've made expanding voting rights a MAJOR priority. Now, Republican operatives are looking to state level politics to try and make it harder for Democrats, young people, and people of color to vote. In Arizona -- a swing state that could determine who holds the White House AND the United States Senate -- Republican legislators are pushing a series of bills that would make it harder to vote.- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - [](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d0792/72412298/44ead486/610089fe/1020719233/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R6YkhWbmZYMD0iOiJ2cHBfbW9udGhseV92b3RpbmdyaWdodHMiLCJlM3QyWlhKemFXOXVmWDA9IjoiVlBQX0VNX0ZSXzE5MDQwNV9BWlZvdGluZ1JpZ2h0c19VMV9YMSIsImUzc2dZWE5ySUgxOSI6IjI1In0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/vpp_monthly_votingrights?refcode=VPP_EM_FR_190405_AZVotingRights_U1_X1&amount=25&recurring=auto&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_FR_190405_AZVotingRights_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_FR_190405_AZVotingRights_U1_X1") Ever since Democrats took back the House, they've made expanding voting rights a MAJOR priority. **Now, Republican operatives are looking to state level politics to try to make it harder for Democrats, young people, and people of color to vote.** In Arizona -- _a swing state that could determine who holds the White House AND the United States Senate_ \-- Republican legislators are pushing a series of bills that would make it harder to vote. name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: PETITION: Save the Violence Against Women Act
body:
We need to act now.
[](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976d8b/2ae112ef/6cef207d/357daee9/2223307201/VEsH/ "https://2020.kirstengillibrand.com/")
[](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976d8b/2ae112ef/6cef207d/357daee9/2223307201/VEsE/ "https://2020.kirstengillibrand.com/")
_**[The NRA is lobbying against the Violence Against Women Act because they want people convicted of domestic abuse to be able to buy guns. Add your name to stand against the NRA now!](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://action.kirstengillibrand.com/kg-VAWA?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk3023-ja-gfp-nd&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fact.kirstengillibrand.com%2Fkg2020-VAWA-priorities")**_
Peter--
The NRA is taking a stand AGAINST the Violence Against Women Act because of a provision that would ban people who have committed domestic abuse from obtaining firearms.
This Act should not be a partisan issue--but it became one when Democrats introduced an amendment to protect women from their abusers and stalkers. The Act would provide funds for programs aimed at preventing domestic violence and would prevent those who have been convicted of domestic violence from buying a gun. And now the NRA wants it gone.
**That's why we've teamed up with Enough is Enough Voter Project to help save the Violence Against Women Act.**
**[Sign the petition: Stand up to the NRA and demand that the Senate pass the Violence Against Women Act.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://action.kirstengillibrand.com/kg-VAWA?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk3023-ja-gfp-nd&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fact.kirstengillibrand.com%2Fkg2020-VAWA-priorities")**
The Violence Against Women Act has protected women and survivors of assault since 1994. It is a crucial piece of legislation that we can't afford to lose. It is expiring and has to be reauthorized.
Now the NRA is threatening to punish lawmakers if they vote for reauthorizing this crucial law that protects women - they have even threatened to dock lawmakers' NRA grades for voting in support of the Act.
But we know the facts:
* A woman is **five times more likely to be killed** if her abuser is a gun owner, and the risk of fatality is five times as high if a gun is involved in a domestic dispute.
* In an average month, **52 American women are fatally shot** by an intimate partner.
* An estimate of **4.5 million American women alive today have been threatened with a gun by an intimate partner.**
* Nearly **1 million women** alive today have been shot or shot at by an intimate partner.

The Violence Against Women Act, and the provision for preventing abusers from owning guns, **will save lives**.
**[Add your name if you agree: Convicted stalkers and domestic abusers should not have access to firearms.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://action.kirstengillibrand.com/kg-VAWA?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk3023-ja-gfp-nd&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fact.kirstengillibrand.com%2Fkg2020-VAWA-priorities")**
Enough is Enough Voter Project was founded in 2018 to hold all elected officials accused of sexual assault and their allies accountable.
Together, Kirsten Gillibrand and the Enough is Enough Voter Project are proudly working to protect and support women. Add your name today to join us in solidarity with survivors of domestic abuse.
| [ADD YOUR NAME](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://action.kirstengillibrand.com/kg-VAWA?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk3023-ja-gfp-nd&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fact.kirstengillibrand.com%2Fkg2020-VAWA-priorities")
---
Thanks,
Kirsten Gillibrand

| **_Next Generation of Female Leaders_** Earlier today, I had the pleasure of sharing a few words at the Richard G. Lugar Excellence in Public Service Series’ Annual Luncheon alongside **Senator Todd Young** , **Congresswoman Susan Brooks** and our special guest, **Congresswoman Elise Stefanik**.

As I said there – and as I’ve said here multiple times – Indiana is on a roll. We’re adding jobs, we’re growing our economy, we’re investing in our infrastructure and we’re making our state safer and healthier for generations to come. And a large reason for that – a main driver of that – is the people who were in the room with us today.

Everywhere I go, every event I attend, I see someone who has either successfully completed the Lugar Series program or is a long-time friend of the program. The state of Indiana has been blessed with so many great leaders over the years – some with titles and some without – and I can say without a doubt that it is a direct result of the work of the Lugar Series in training, developing and equipping the next generation of female leaders.

-Kyle Hupfer

**PHOTO:** _**Indiana GOP Chairman Kyle Hupfer** delivers keynote remarks at the Elkhart County Lincoln Day Dinner, where he talks about the incredible position we're in as a party in all corners of Indiana!_
---
|
---
| | | **GOVERNOR HOLCOMB'S WEEK THAT WAS**
---
| [ ](http://)
---
| **_Governor Holcomb’s Next Level Agenda Advances_** It was another busy week at the Statehouse, as several key legislative items in **Governor Eric Holcomb’s** 2019 Next Level Agenda – all focused on practical and people-centered solutions – made major progress through the Indiana General Assembly. This included:
* **Standing Against Bias Crimes** : Indiana’s new bias crimes law allows judges to enhance sentences on criminals who commit crimes because of an aggravating bias that targets a group. The Indiana Senate concurred with amendments made in the Indiana House, and Governor Holcomb signed the bill into law.
* **Supporting Our Veterans:** This bill would exempt a portion of military pensions from state income tax, making Indiana an even more attractive place for veterans to live once their military service has concluded. The Senate Appropriations Committee voted in favor of the bill, and it will now be heard by the full Senate.
* **Attacking the Drug Epidemic** : This bill puts regulations in place to ensure Hoosiers struggling with dependency have access to treatment, while also ensuring the treatment is appropriate and professional. The Indiana Senate concurred with amendments made in the Indiana House, and the bill is heading to Governor Holcomb’s desk.
* **Skilling-Up Our Workforce:** This bill doubles down on Indiana’s Next Level Jobs initiatives that is helping to develop Indiana’s skilled workforce, in addition to giving communities the tools to develop local education-to-work pipelines. It also removes barriers that make it harder for schools to partner directly with local Hoosier businesses and industry leaders on technical and vocational education. The Senate Appropriations Committee voted in favor of the bill, and it will now be heard by the full Senate.

There are still several weeks to go during this year’s legislative session, so follow Governor Holcomb’s campaign social media channels on [Facebook](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=a38b3a6393&e=1acd9bb3d6), [Twitter](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=eb05c257eb&e=1acd9bb3d6) and [Instagram](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=be7811a15a&e=1acd9bb3d6) to keep up-to-date on the legislative session and the governor’s Next Level Agenda!

| **_Indiana is Off the List & On the Map!_** This week, **Governor Eric Holcomb** made history by signing Indiana's first-ever bias crimes law – getting Indiana off the list (of just five other states) without a law and on the map for businesses and future Hoosiers looking for a place to locate! Here’s what you need to know:
* **What They’re Saying:******[Read this collection](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=30ea106ef8&e=1acd9bb3d6) of what Indiana’s top leaders are saying
* **Editorial:******[Bias Crimes law hits the right note for our state](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=fcb7edf6d0&e=1acd9bb3d6)
* **Former Indiana Supreme Court Justice:** Indiana’s new bias crimes law [covers more than just the list](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c05d74e97f&e=1acd9bb3d6)

| **_Beat the Governor Bracket Challenge:_**
_******Heartbreaking Loss for Boilermakers**_ It’s almost Final Four time! We all remember that **Governor Holcomb** picked Indiana’s only team in the tournament – the Purdue Boilermakers – to take home the national championship. And with Purdue’s heartbreaking loss last weekend, unfortunately all of **Governor Holcomb’s** remaining bracket selections are now out of the running.

With his bracket falling back to 30th place in our Beat the Governor Bracket Challenge, [check out the standings here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=2d84c008f2&e=1acd9bb3d6) to see the full list of Hoosier hoops fans who are now beating **Governor Holcomb** with their brackets. Remember, the top three finishers at the end of our Beat the Governor Bracket Challenge will win either a basketball signed by Governor Holcomb or an item from our [Indiana GOP store](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=a14a47d2ae&e=1acd9bb3d6).

| **_Get to Know Nappanee Mayor Phil Jenkins_** It’s a municipal election year in Indiana! Across the state, we’ve got Republican elected officials who are working hard to deliver results for their communities, and one of them is [Nappanee Mayor Phil Jenkins](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=2b7e2fd7ec&e=1acd9bb3d6). As a registered architect and former city council member, he brings a unique approach to solving issues facing Nappanee, a city in northern Indiana. He was first elected mayor in 2015, and this year he’s up for re-election.

Learn more about **Nappanee Mayor Phil Jenkins** :
---
| | [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=71da51dc4a&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
**Q: What makes Nappanee unique?**
A: We’re home to one-of-a-kind shops with genuine, caring residents.

**Q: What’s your vision for Nappanee in 10 years?**
A: I’m focused on helping build:
* A steadily growing population
* A more diverse and resilient economy
* Reinvestment in our core residential neighborhoods
* A robust, interconnected park system with improved amenities
* Upgrades to our road and utility infrastructure
* A fiber optic network throughout the entire city
* A physically and mentally healthier community

**Q: What’s your plan to help grow Nappanee’s economy?**
A: I’m focused on private-public partnerships to mutually benefit private business and the community.
---
| **Q: If you could change one thing about Nappanee, what would it be and why?**
A: It’s critical that we continue to work to build stronger families. As a mentor in our school system, I see the need for reaching out to our struggling families and providing the support and resources necessary to ensure every child has the best environment to reach their full potential.

Lugar Excellence in Public Service Series’ Annual Luncheon alongside... | | | | | | http:// | Next Generation of Female Leaders Earlier today, I had the pleasure of sharing a few words at the Richard G. Lugar Excellence in Public Service Series’ Annual Luncheon alongside Senator Todd Young , Congresswoman Susan Brooks and our special guest, Congresswoman Elise Stefanik. As I said there – and as I’ve said here multiple times – Indiana is on a roll. We’re adding jobs, we’re growing our economy, we’re investing in our infrastructure and we’re making our state safer and healthier for generations to come. And a large reason for that – a main driver of that – is the people who were in the room with us today. Everywhere I go, every event I attend, I see someone who has either successfully completed the Lugar Series program or is a long-time friend of the program. The state of Indiana has been blessed with so many great leaders over the years – some with titles and some without – and I can say without a doubt that it is a direct result of the work of the Lugar Series in training, developing and equipping the next generation of female leaders. -Kyle Hupfer PHOTO: Indiana GOP Chairman Kyle Hupfer delivers keynote remarks at the Elkhart County Lincoln Day Dinner, where he talks about the incredible position we're in as a party in all corners of Indiana! | | | | GOVERNOR HOLCOMB'S WEEK THAT WAS | http:// | Governor Holcomb’s Next Level Agenda Advances It was another busy week at the Statehouse, as several key legislative items in Governor Eric Holcomb’s 2019 Next Level Agenda – all focused on practical and people-centered solutions – made major progress through the Indiana General Assembly. This included: Standing Against Bias Crimes : Indiana’s new bias crimes law allows judges to enhance sentences on criminals who commit crimes because of an aggravating bias that targets a group. The Indiana Senate concurred with amendments made in the Indiana House, and Governor Holcomb signed the bill into law. Supporting Our Veterans: This bill would exempt a portion of military pensions from state income tax, making Indiana an even more attractive place for veterans to live once their military service has concluded. The Senate Appropriations Committee voted in favor of the bill, and it will now be heard by the full Senate. Attacking the Drug Epidemic : This bill puts regulations in place to ensure Hoosiers struggling with dependency have access to treatment, while also ensuring the treatment is appropriate and professional. The Indiana Senate concurred with amendments made in the Indiana House, and the bill is heading to Governor Holcomb’s desk. Skilling-Up Our Workforce: This bill doubles down on Indiana’s Next Level Jobs initiatives that is helping to develop Indiana’s skilled workforce, in addition to giving communities the tools to develop local education-to-work pipelines. It also removes barriers that make it harder for schools to partner directly with local Hoosier businesses and industry leaders on technical and vocational education. The Senate Appropriations Committee voted in favor of the bill, and it will now be heard by the full Senate. There are still several weeks to go during this year’s legislative session, so follow Governor Holcomb’s campaign social media channels on , and to keep up-to-date on the legislative session and the governor’s Next Level Agenda! | | http:// | Indiana is Off the List & On the Map! And with Purdue’s heartbreaking loss last weekend, unfortunately all of Governor Holcomb’s remaining bracket selections are now out of the running.
